source_paper_title: Convergence Analysis of Accelerated Stochastic Gradient Descent Under the Growth Condition
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Chen_2024, title={Convergence Analysis of Accelerated Stochastic Gradient Descent Under the Growth Condition}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2021.0293}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2021.0293}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Chen, You-Lin and Na, Sen and Kolar, Mladen}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2492–2526} }
context:
Page 27, Section 7 (Conclusion)

We do not provide a lower bound analysis for NAM, RMM, iDAM+, and, therefore, cannot conclude that these methods do not improve the convergence over SGD when \(\delta \ge 1\). ... One of the limitations of our work is the sharpness of the analysis. Due to the lack of lower bound analysis on \(\delta\), it is unclear whether the condition of having a small \(\delta\) for different methods is necessary for acceleration; and how sharp such a condition is.
title: Lower bounds on acceleration thresholds for stochastic momentum methods under growth condition
background_latex:
Let $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ be continuously differentiable, $L$-smooth and $m$-strongly convex (with respect to the Euclidean norm $\|\cdot\|$), with condition number $\kappa:=L/m$, and unique minimizer $x^*\in\arg\min_x f(x)$. A stochastic first-order oracle returns a noisy gradient
\[
 g(x)=\nabla f(x)+\varepsilon(x),
\]
where $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon(x)\mid x]=0$. The noise is assumed to satisfy the growth condition: there exist constants $\delta\ge 0$ and $\sigma^2\ge 0$ such that for all $x$,
\[
\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2.
\]
Consider the following accelerated stochastic gradient-type methods with (constant) algorithm parameters:
1) Nesterov’s accelerated method (NAM), an iteration that uses a momentum parameter and evaluates stochastic gradients at a momentum-extrapolated point.
2) Robust momentum method (RMM), an iteration that updates a momentum variable and a primary iterate using fixed parameters.
3) Implicit accelerated dual averaging (iDAM+), a dual-averaging-based accelerated scheme.
For each method, one is interested in whether it can achieve an accelerated linear bias decay of the form $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (or equivalently a per-iteration contraction factor $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) under the growth condition when the multiplicative noise level $\delta$ is not small (e.g., $\delta\ge 1$), compared with the non-accelerated $1-\Theta(1/\kappa)$ rate typical of constant-stepsize SGD.
problem_definition_latex:
For NAM, RMM, and iDAM+ under the growth condition, determine (prove) sharp necessary conditions on the multiplicative noise parameter $\delta$ for acceleration over SGD. In particular, establish a lower bound (impossibility result) showing whether there exists a threshold $\delta_0$ such that for some $\delta\ge \delta_0$ and some $(m,L)$-smooth strongly convex objective $f$ together with an oracle satisfying
\(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2\),
no choice of constant algorithm parameters for these methods can guarantee an accelerated bias decay rate $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (equivalently contraction $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) uniformly over all such problem instances.
latex:
Let $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ be continuously differentiable, $L$-smooth and $m$-strongly convex (with respect to the Euclidean norm $\|\cdot\|$), with condition number $\kappa:=L/m$, and unique minimizer $x^*\in\arg\min_x f(x)$. A stochastic first-order oracle returns a noisy gradient
\[
 g(x)=\nabla f(x)+\varepsilon(x),
\]
where $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon(x)\mid x]=0$. The noise is assumed to satisfy the growth condition: there exist constants $\delta\ge 0$ and $\sigma^2\ge 0$ such that for all $x$,
\[
\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2.
\]
Consider the following accelerated stochastic gradient-type methods with (constant) algorithm parameters:
1) Nesterov’s accelerated method (NAM), an iteration that uses a momentum parameter and evaluates stochastic gradients at a momentum-extrapolated point.
2) Robust momentum method (RMM), an iteration that updates a momentum variable and a primary iterate using fixed parameters.
3) Implicit accelerated dual averaging (iDAM+), a dual-averaging-based accelerated scheme.
For each method, one is interested in whether it can achieve an accelerated linear bias decay of the form $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (or equivalently a per-iteration contraction factor $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) under the growth condition when the multiplicative noise level $\delta$ is not small (e.g., $\delta\ge 1$), compared with the non-accelerated $1-\Theta(1/\kappa)$ rate typical of constant-stepsize SGD.

For NAM, RMM, and iDAM+ under the growth condition, determine (prove) sharp necessary conditions on the multiplicative noise parameter $\delta$ for acceleration over SGD. In particular, establish a lower bound (impossibility result) showing whether there exists a threshold $\delta_0$ such that for some $\delta\ge \delta_0$ and some $(m,L)$-smooth strongly convex objective $f$ together with an oracle satisfying
\(\mathbb{E}\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2 \le \delta\,\|\nabla f(x)\|^2 + \sigma^2\),
no choice of constant algorithm parameters for these methods can guarantee an accelerated bias decay rate $\exp(-k/c\sqrt{\kappa})$ (equivalently contraction $1-\Theta(1/\sqrt{\kappa})$) uniformly over all such problem instances.
open_problem_quote: We do not provide a lower bound analysis for NAM, RMM, iDAM+, and, therefore, cannot conclude that these methods do not improve the convergence over SGD when \(\delta \ge 1\). ... One of the limitations of our work is the sharpness of the analysis. Due to the lack of lower bound analysis on \(\delta\), it is unclear whether the condition of having a small \(\delta\) for different methods is necessary for acceleration; and how sharp such a condition is.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can search over low-dimensional strongly convex quadratics with parametrized multiplicative-noise oracles to try to exhibit instances where a fixed method/parameterization fails to beat SGD, and performance metrics (contraction factors) are computable. However, proving necessity uniformly over all instances and over all parameter choices is harder than finding a single failing instance.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be represented programmatically (e.g., quadratics plus oracle noise models) and evaluated via simulated convergence rates, enabling evolutionary search over instances and parameter settings to maximize the acceleration gap. This can generate strong empirical evidence and conjectures about sharp thresholds in $\delta$, though turning them into rigorous lower bounds still requires theory.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Chen–Na–Kolar, 2023) frames acceleration under the growth condition \(\mathbb E\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2\le \delta\|\nabla f(x)\|^2+\sigma^2\) as a bias–variance tradeoff in a unified recursion of the form \(\mathbb E V_{k+1}\le (1-c_1(\delta)/\sqrt\kappa)\,\mathbb E V_k + c_2(\delta)\sigma^2\). For constant parameters, it proves *sufficient* \(\delta\)-regimes for accelerated linear bias decay: NAM and iDAM+ accelerate only for \(\delta<1\), RMM for \(\delta<1/4\), while DAM+ can be tuned to accelerate for all \(\delta\ge 0\). The paper explicitly notes that these \(\delta\)-restrictions are not known to be necessary and calls for lower-bound/impossibility results.

Among the forward-citing works provided, none resolves the requested sharp necessary conditions. The Markovian-noise paper establishes acceleration and lower bounds in a different model (Markov dependence plus almost-sure strong-growth and batching), while the interpolation/strong-growth estimating-sequences paper is about upper bounds and flags a bug for standard momentum forms. The RAAS work shows acceleration for an adaptive method under bounded-moment oracle assumptions, again not addressing impossibility thresholds for constant-parameter NAM/RMM/iDAM+.

Therefore, based on the supplied forward-citation set, the problem of proving sharp lower bounds on the multiplicative-noise threshold \(\delta\) that precludes accelerated bias decay for NAM, RMM, and iDAM+ under the (in-expectation) growth condition remains open. Promising directions suggested by the source paper’s discussion include constructing hard strongly convex instances (e.g., least-squares-type quadratics with large effective \(\delta\)) and proving that for \(\delta\) above a method-dependent constant, the best achievable uniform contraction reverts to \(1-\Theta(1/\kappa)\) for any fixed-parameter momentum tuning.

sources.title: First order methods with markovian noise: from acceleration to variational inequalities
sources.theorem_or_result:
In the Markovian-noise setting (uniformly geometrically ergodic chain with mixing time \(\tau\)) and an almost-sure “growth/strong growth” bound \(\|\nabla F(x,z)-\nabla f(x)\|^2\le \sigma^2+\delta^2\|\nabla f(x)\|^2\), the paper analyzes a randomized-batching Nesterov-type accelerated SGD (Algorithm 1) and proves a linear convergence bound of the form
\[
\mathbb E\big[\|x_N-x^*\|^2 + \tfrac{6}{\mu}(f(x_N^f)-f(x^*))\big]
\le \exp\Big(-\tfrac{N}{3}\sqrt{p^2\mu\alpha}\Big)\,\mathcal E_0
+ \tfrac{p}{\mu^{3/2}\sqrt{\alpha}}\big(\delta^2\tau b^{-1}+\sigma^2\tau^2 b^{-2}\big),
\]
for suitable constant parameters \(p,\alpha\) and batching parameters \(b,M,B\) (Theorem 1), yielding oracle complexity \(\tilde O\big(\tau[(1+\delta^2)\sqrt{L/\mu}\log(1/\varepsilon)+\sigma^2/(\mu^2\varepsilon)]\big)\) (Corollary 1). It also gives Markovian lower bounds \(\Omega(\tau\sqrt{L/\mu}\log(1/\varepsilon)+\tau\sigma^2/(\mu^2\varepsilon))\) under these assumptions (Theorem 2).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for sharp necessary conditions (lower bounds/impossibility thresholds) on the multiplicative noise level \(\delta\) under the (expectation) growth condition for *acceleration* of specific constant-parameter momentum methods (NAM, RMM, iDAM+), i.e., when accelerated bias decay \(\exp(-k/c\sqrt\kappa)\) is impossible beyond some \(\delta_0\).

This paper does not establish such a \(\delta\)-threshold impossibility result for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ under the growth condition. Instead it studies a different noise model (Markovian dependence) and assumes an almost-sure bound \(\|
abla F-
abla f\|^2\le \sigma^2+\delta^2\|
abla f\|^2\) (stronger than the open problem’s conditional second-moment bound), and it achieves acceleration via *randomized batching* (not the plain constant-batch NAM/RMM/iDAM+). Its lower bounds concern unavoidable dependence on mixing time \(	au\) and on the multiplicative parameter in this Markov/strong-growth model, but they do not rule out acceleration for large \(\delta\) in the i.i.d. growth-condition setting nor provide sharp necessary conditions for the three methods named in the open problem.

sources.title: Faster convergence of stochastic accelerated gradient descent under interpolation
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proposes an estimating-sequences analysis for a generalized stochastic AGD scheme (Eq. (5)) and claims (but later retracts for standard momentum S-AGD) accelerated rates under strong growth: if a primal update satisfies the expected progress condition \(\mathbb{E}[f(w_{k+1})]\le f(y_k)-	frac{\eta_k}{2}\|
abla f(y_k)\|^2\) and \(f\) is \(L\)-smooth and \(\mu\)-strongly convex, then for the generalized scheme \(\mathbb{E}[f(w_{k+1})]-f(w^*)\le \prod_{i=0}^k(1-\sqrt{\eta_i\mu})\,(f(w_0)-f(w^*)+	frac{\mu}{2}\|w_0-w^*\|^2)\). Specializing with \(\eta=1/(ho L)\) under strong growth \(\mathbb{E}\|
abla f(w,z)\|^2\le ho\|
abla f(w)\|^2\) yields a claimed linear rate \((1-\sqrt{\mu/(ho L)})^{k}\) for Eq. (5), but the paper explicitly states this does not hold for standard stochastic AGD/momentum (Eq. (6)) due to a bug.
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp necessary conditions (lower bounds/impossibility thresholds) on multiplicative noise \(\delta\) under the growth condition for acceleration of specific momentum/accelerated methods (NAM, RMM, iDAM+). This paper is about sufficient conditions and upper-bound rates under the strong growth condition (a special case of the growth condition with \(\sigma^2=0\) and \(\delta=\rho-1\) in spirit), and it does not provide any lower bounds or impossibility thresholds. Moreover, the main claimed improvement (dependence \(\sqrt{\rho}\) rather than \(\rho\)) for standard stochastic Nesterov/momentum is explicitly invalidated by the authors’ bug note; they even suggest a possible lower bound might show \(\rho\) is tight. Thus it does not resolve the threshold question, but it is tangentially relevant as it highlights the difficulty of improving dependence on multiplicative noise and points toward the need for lower bounds.

sources.title: Robust Accelerated Adaptive Search: High-Probability Complexity Bounds under Bounded-Moment Stochastic Oracles
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proposes RAAS, a stochastic accelerated adaptive step-search method using both a stochastic first-order oracle (SFO) and a stochastic zeroth-order oracle (SZO), and proves high-probability stopping-time complexity bounds under finite-moment oracle assumptions. In the strongly convex case (Assumption 6), for any target accuracy 
\(\varepsilon>\varepsilon'_\varphi\), Theorem 2 shows \(\mathbb P\{T_\varepsilon\le t\}\ge 1-\text{(poly/exponential tail)}\) provided 
\[t=O\!\left(\frac{1}{C_\kappa(\hat p-1/2)}\log\frac{\Phi_0}{\varepsilon}\right),\qquad C_\kappa=-\ln\bigl(1-(1-\vartheta)\sqrt{2\theta\mu\bar\gamma}\bigr),\]
with \(\bar\gamma=\Theta(1/L)\) so \(C_\kappa=\Theta(\kappa^{-1/2})\), yielding an accelerated order \(T_\varepsilon=O(\sqrt\kappa\log(\Phi_0/\varepsilon))\) down to a noise-determined floor \(\varepsilon'_\varphi>0\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp necessary conditions (lower bounds/impossibility thresholds) on the multiplicative noise parameter \(\delta\) in the growth condition \(\mathbb E\|\varepsilon(x)\|^2\le \delta\|\nabla f(x)\|^2+\sigma^2\) that preclude accelerated linear bias decay for fixed-parameter momentum methods (NAM/RMM/iDAM+). This paper does not study the growth-condition model nor the same algorithms: its SFO assumptions bound absolute gradient estimation error moments (e.g., \(\mathbb E_t\|G_t-\nabla\varphi(\hat y_t)\|^2\le \epsilon_g^2\)) and allow bias/heavy tails, and it uses an adaptive step-search with extra SZO queries and tunable momentum intervention \(\vartheta\). It provides sufficient conditions for RAAS to achieve accelerated \(O(\sqrt\kappa\log(1/\varepsilon))\) stopping-time complexity above a noise floor, but it does not derive impossibility results or lower bounds on \(\delta\) for NAM/RMM/iDAM+ under the growth condition.
